question_paper_html:
Annual Exam - 2019

Code: 65/1/3 (set-3)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 11 printed pages.
  • Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 29 questions.
  • Please write down the Serial Number of the question before attempting it.
  • 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:100

General Instructions :

  1. All questions are compulsory.
  2. This question paper contains 29 questions divided into four sections A, B, C and D. Section A comprises of 4 questions of one mark each, Section B comprises of 8 questions of two marks each, Section C comprises of 11 questions of four marks each and Section D comprises of 6 questions of six marks each.
  3. All questions in Section A are to be answered in one word, one sentence or as per the exact requirement of the question.
  4. There is no overall choice. However, internal choice has been provided in 1 question of Section A, 3 questions of Section B, 3 questions of Section C and 3 questions of Section D. You have to attempt only one of the alternatives in all such questions.
  5. Use of calculators is not permitted. You may ask logarithmic tables, if required.

SECTION - A

Question numbers 1 to 4 carry 1 mark each.

1.

If 3A - B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 5 & 0 \\ 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}and B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 4 & 3 \\ 2 & 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, then find the matrix A.
2.

Write the order and the degree of the following differential equation:
{tex}x^3\left(\frac{\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{d} x^2}\right)^2+x\left(\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}\right)^4{/tex} = 0
3.

If f(x) = x + 1, find {tex}\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} (fof)(x).
4.

If a line makes anlges 90o, 135o, 45o with the x, y and z axes respectively, find its direction cosines.

OR

Find the vector equation of the line which passes through the point (3, 4, 5) and is parallel to the vector {tex}2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-3 \hat{k}{/tex}.

SECTION - B

Question numbers 5 to 12 carry 2 marks each.

5.

Find: {tex}\int \sin x \cdot \log \cos x \ d x{/tex}.
6.

Evaluate: {tex}\int\limits_{-\pi}^\pi\left(1-x^2\right) \sin x \cos ^2 x \mathrm{~d} x{/tex}.

OR

Evaluate: {tex}\int_\limits{-1}^2 \frac{|x|}{x} d x {/tex}
7.

Examine whether the operation * defined on R by a * b = ab + 1 is

  1. a binary or not.
  2. if a binary operation, is it associative or not?
8.

Find a matrix A such that 2A - 3B + 5C = O, where B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-2} & {2} & {0} \\ {3} & {1} & {4}\end{array}\right]{/tex} and C = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{2} & {0} & {-2} \\ {7} & {1} & {6}\end{array}\right]{/tex}.
9.

A die marked 1, 2, 3 in red and 4, 5, 6 in green is tossed. Let A be the event number is even and B be the event number is marked red. Find whether the events A and B are independent or not.
10.

Form the differential equation representing the family of curves y = e2x (a + bx), where a and b are arbitrary constants.
11.

A die is thrown 6 times. If getting an odd number is a success, what is the probability of (i) 5 successes? (ii) atmost 5 successes?

OR

The random variable X has a probability distribution P(X) of the following form, where k is some number.
P(X = x) = {tex}\left\{\begin{array}{cl} \mathrm{k}, & \text { if } x=0 \\ 2 \mathrm{k}, & \text { if } x=1 \\ 3 \mathrm{k}, & \text { if } x=2 \\ 0, & \text { otherwise } \end{array}\right.{/tex}
Determine the value of k.
12.

If the sum of two unit vectors is a unit vector, prove that the magnitude of their difference is {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}.

OR

If {tex}\vec{a}=2 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+\hat{k},{/tex} {tex}\vec{b}=\hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=-3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex}, find {tex}[\vec{\mathrm{a}} \vec{\mathrm{b}} \vec{\mathrm{c}}]{/tex}.

SECTION - C

Question numbers 13 to 23 carry 4 marks each.

13.

Using properties of determinants, prove the following:
{tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} a & b & c \\ a-b & b-c & c-a \\ b+c & c+a & a+b \end{array}\right|{/tex} = a3 + b3 + c3 - 3abc.
14.

Solve: tan-1 4x + tan-1 6x = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}.
15.

Show that the relation R in R defined as R = {(a, b) : a {tex}\leq{/tex} b}, is reflexive and transitive but not symmetric.

OR

Prove that the function f : N {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} N, defined by f(x) = x2 + x + 1 is one-one but not onto. Find inverse of f : N {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} S, where S is range of f.
16.

Find the equation of tangent to the curve {tex}y=\sqrt{3 x-2}{/tex} which is parallel to the line 4x - 2y + 5 = 0. Also, write the equation of normal to the curve at the point of contact.
17.

If log (x2 + y2) = 2 tan-1 {tex}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex}, show that {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x+y}{x-y}{/tex}

OR

If xy - yx = ab, find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}.
18.

If y = (sin-1x) 2, prove that {tex}\left(1-x^2\right) \frac{\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{d} x^2}-x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}-2{/tex} = 0.
19.

Prove that {tex}\int\limits_{0}^{\mathfrak{a}}{/tex} f(x) dx = {tex}\int\limits_{0}^{\mathfrak{a}}{/tex} f(a - x) dx. hence evaluate {tex}\int\limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x \sin x}{1+\cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex}.
20.

Find: {tex}\int \frac{\cos x}{(1+\sin x)(2+\sin x)} d x{/tex}
21.

Solve the differential equation: {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}-\frac{2 x}{1+x^2} \mathrm{y}{/tex} = x2 + 2

OR

Solve the differential equation: (x + 1){tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}{/tex} = 2e-y - 1; y(0) = 0.
22.

If {tex}\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}, 2 \hat{i}+5 \hat{j}, 3 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-3 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{i}-6 \hat{j}-\hat{k} {/tex} respectively are the position vectors of points A, B, C and D, then find the angle between the straight lines AB and CD. Find whether {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AB}}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{CD}}{/tex} are collinear or not.
23.

Find the value of {tex}\lambda{/tex}, so that the lines {tex}\frac{1-x}{3}=\frac{7 y-14}{\lambda}=\frac{z-3}{2}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{7-7 x}{3 \lambda}=\frac{y-5}{1}=\frac{6-z}{5}{/tex} are at right angles. Also, find whether the lines are intersecting or not.

SECTION - D

Question numbers 24 to 29 carry 6 marks each.

24.

A tank with rectangular base and rectangular sides, open at the top is to be constructed so that its depth is 2 m and volume is 8 m3. If building of tank costs Rs 70 per sq.metres for the base and Rs 45 per sq. metre for sides. What is the cost of least expensive tank?
25.

If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 & 2 \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find A–1 and hence solve the following system of linear equations:
x + y + z = 6
x + 2z = 7
3x + y + z = 12

OR

Find the inverse of the following matrix using elementary operations. {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 2 & -2 \\ -1 & 3 & 0 \\ 0 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}.
26.

Prove that the curves y2 = 4x and x2 = 4y divide the area of the square bounded by sides x = 0, x = 4, y = 4 and y = 0 into three equal parts.

OR

Using integration, find the area of the triangle whose vertices are (2, 3), (3, 5) and (4, 4).
27.

A manufacturer has employed 5 skilled men and 10 semi-skilled men and makes two models A and B of an article. The making of one item of model A requires 2 hours work by a skilled man and 2 hours work by a semi-skilled man. One item of model B requires 1 hour by a skilled man and 3 hours by a semi-skilled man. No man is expected to work more than 8 hours per day. The manufacturer’s profit on an item of model A is ₹15 and on an item of model B is ₹10. How many of items of each model should be made per day in order to maximize daily profit? Formulate the above LPP and solve it graphically and find the maximum profit.
28.

Find the vector and Cartesian equations of the plane passing through the points (2, 2, -1), (3, 4, 2) and (7, 0, 6). Also find the vector equation of a plane passing through (4, 3, 1) and parallel to the plane obtained above.

OR

Find the vector equation of the plane that contains the lines {tex}\vec{r}=(\hat{i}+\hat{j})+\lambda(\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}){/tex} and the point (-1, 3, -4). Also, find the length of the perpendicular drawn from the point (2, 1, 4) to the plane thus obtained.
29.

Two cards are drawn simultaneously (or successively without replacement) from a well shuffled pack of 52 cards. Find the mean and variance of the number of kings.

answer_key_html:
Annual Exam - 2019

Code: 65/1/3 (set-3)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 11 printed pages.
  • Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 29 questions.
  • Please write down the Serial Number of the question before attempting it.
  • 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:100

General Instructions :

  1. All questions are compulsory.
  2. This question paper contains 29 questions divided into four sections A, B, C and D. Section A comprises of 4 questions of one mark each, Section B comprises of 8 questions of two marks each, Section C comprises of 11 questions of four marks each and Section D comprises of 6 questions of six marks each.
  3. All questions in Section A are to be answered in one word, one sentence or as per the exact requirement of the question.
  4. There is no overall choice. However, internal choice has been provided in 1 question of Section A, 3 questions of Section B, 3 questions of Section C and 3 questions of Section D. You have to attempt only one of the alternatives in all such questions.
  5. Use of calculators is not permitted. You may ask logarithmic tables, if required.

SECTION - A

Question numbers 1 to 4 carry 1 mark each.

1.

If 3A - B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 5 & 0 \\ 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}and B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 4 & 3 \\ 2 & 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, then find the matrix A.

Solution:

Given 3A - B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 5 & 0 \\ 1 & 1 \end{array}\right], {/tex} {tex}B=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 4 & 3 \\ 2 & 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
or 3A - {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 4 & 3 \\ 2 & 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ll} 5 & 0 \\ 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
or 3A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 5 & 0 \\ 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}{tex}+\left[\begin{array}{ll} 4 & 3 \\ 2 & 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
or 3A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 9 & 3 \\ 3 & 6 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
or A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}

 1
2.

Write the order and the degree of the following differential equation:
{tex}x^3\left(\frac{\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{d} x^2}\right)^2+x\left(\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}\right)^4{/tex} = 0

Solution:

In the given equation, the highest-order derivative is {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex} and its power is 2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} its order = 2 and degree = 2.

 1
3.

If f(x) = x + 1, find {tex}\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} (fof)(x).

Solution:

We have, f(x) = x + 1
Now, (fof)(x) = f(f(x))
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (fof)(x) = f(x + 1)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (fof)(x) = (x + 1) + 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {(fof)(x)} = x + 2
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{d x}\{(\text { fof })(x)\}=\frac{d}{d x}(x)+\frac{d}{d x}(2){/tex}  [differentiating both sides with respect x]
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{d x}\{(\text { fof })(x)\}=1+0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{d x}\{(\text { fof })(x)\}=1{/tex}

 1
4.

If a line makes anlges 90o, 135o, 45o with the x, y and z axes respectively, find its direction cosines.

Solution:

Direction cosines of a line making angle {tex}\alpha{/tex} with x-axis, {tex}\beta{/tex} with y-axis and {tex}\gamma{/tex} with z-axis are l, m ,n.
l = cos{tex}\alpha{/tex} , m = cos{tex}\beta{/tex} , n = cos{tex}\gamma{/tex}
Here, {tex}\alpha{/tex} = 90° , {tex}\beta{/tex} = 135° and {tex}\gamma{/tex} = 45°
l = cos 90°
=0
m = cos 135°
= cos(180° - 45°)
= -cos 45°
{tex}=-\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex}
= cos 45°
{tex}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex}

 1

OR

Find the vector equation of the line which passes through the point (3, 4, 5) and is parallel to the vector {tex}2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-3 \hat{k}{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}=(3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+4 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+5 \hat{\mathrm{k}})+\lambda(2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex}

 1

SECTION - B

Question numbers 5 to 12 carry 2 marks each.

5.

Find: {tex}\int \sin x \cdot \log \cos x \ d x{/tex}.

Solution:

I = {tex}\int \sin x \cdot \log (\cos x) \ d x{/tex}
cos x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}-\int \log t \cdot 1 d t{/tex}
= {tex}-\left[\mathrm{t} \cdot \log \mathrm{t}-\int \frac{1}{\mathrm{t}} \cdot \mathrm{tdt}\right]{/tex}
= t(1 - log t) + C = cos x(1 - log(cos x)) + C

 2
6.

Evaluate: {tex}\int\limits_{-\pi}^\pi\left(1-x^2\right) \sin x \cos ^2 x \mathrm{~d} x{/tex}.

Solution:

Let f(x) = (1 - x2){tex}\cdot{/tex}sin x cos2 x
as f(-x) = -f(x) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f is odd function.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} I = 0

 2

OR

Evaluate: {tex}\int_\limits{-1}^2 \frac{|x|}{x} d x {/tex}

Solution:

Let I = {tex}\int_\limits{-1}^2 \frac{|x|}{x} d x{/tex}
Since, {tex}\frac{|x|}{x}=\left\{\begin{array}{l} \frac{-x}{x}, x<0 \\ \frac{x}{x}, x>0 \end{array}\right.{/tex}
{tex}=\left\{\begin{array}{l} -1, x<0 \\ 1, x>0 \end{array}\right.{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} I {tex}=\int_\limits{-1}^0(-1) d x+\int_\limits0^2(1) d x{/tex}
{tex}=[-x]_{-1}^0+[x]_0^2{/tex}
= -[0 - (-1)] + (2 - 0)
= -1 + 2
= 1

 2
7.

Examine whether the operation * defined on R by a * b = ab + 1 is

  1. a binary or not.
  2. if a binary operation, is it associative or not?
Solution:

Here, a {tex}*{/tex} b = ab+1

  1. Given operation is a*b = ab+1
    If any operation is a binary operation, it must follow closure property. Let a{tex}\in{/tex}R and b{tex}\in{/tex}R Then ab{tex}\in{/tex}R. Also ab+1 {tex}\in{/tex} R So, a*b ∈R. A die marked 1, 2, 3 in red and 4, 5, 6 in green is tossed. Let A be the event number is even and B be the event number is marked red. Find whether the events A and B are independent or not.

Solution:

When a die is thrown, the sample space is
S = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} n(S) = 6
Also, A : number is even and B : number Is red
{tex}\therefore{/tex} A = {2, 4, 6} and B = {1, 2, 3} and A{tex}\cap{/tex}B = {2}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} n(A) = 3, n (B) = 3 and n(A{tex}\cap{/tex}B) = 1
Now, P(A) = {tex}\frac { n ( A ) } { n ( S ) } = \frac { 3 } { 6 } = \frac { 1 } { 2 }{/tex}     
P(B) ={tex}\frac { n ( B ) } { n ( S ) } = \frac { 3 } { 6 } = \frac { 1 } { 2 }{/tex}     
and P(A{tex}\cap{/tex}B) = {tex}\frac { n ( A \cap B ) } { n ( S ) } = \frac { 1 } { 6 }{/tex} 
Now, P(A) {tex}\times{/tex} P(B) = {tex}\frac { 1 } { 2 } \times \frac { 1 } { 2 }{/tex}
 {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 4 } \neq \frac { 1 } { 6 } = P ( A \cap B ){/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad P ( A \cap B ) \neq P ( A ) \times P ( B ){/tex}
Thus, A and B are not independent events.

R1 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R1 + R3 and taking a + b + c common from R1

{tex} \Rightarrow \quad x_{1}-x_{2}=0 {/tex} {tex}\left[\because x_{1}-x_{2}+1 \neq 0\right]{/tex}

f is one-one.
For Onto:

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x(x + 1) = 0
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 0,-1 {tex}\in{/tex} N
Not possible since domain = N
So, f is not onto.
Hence, f is one-one but not onto. Hence Proved
Now, f: N {tex}\rightarrow {/tex}S: f(x)=x2 + x + 1
where S = range (Given)
f: N {tex}\rightarrow {/tex} S is onto as co-domain = range.
Hence, f is invertible.
Let {tex}y=f(x) \Rightarrow y=x^{2}+x+1{/tex}

Solution:

Let {tex} \theta {/tex} be the angle between the lines AB and CD. Then, {tex} \theta {/tex} is also the angle between vectors {tex} \overrightarrow{A B} \text { and } \overrightarrow{C D} {/tex}

and,
 {tex} \overrightarrow{C D}=(\hat{i}-6 \hat{j}-\hat{k})-(3 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-3 \hat{k})=-2 \hat{i}-8 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k} {/tex}
{tex} \therefore|\overrightarrow{A B}|=\sqrt{1+16+1}=3 \sqrt{2},|\overrightarrow{C D}|=\sqrt{4+64+4}=6 \sqrt{2} {/tex}
{tex} \text { and, } (\overrightarrow{A B} \cdot \overrightarrow{C D})=(\hat{i}+4 \hat{j}-\hat{k}) \cdot(-2 \hat{i}-8 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}) {/tex} = - 2 - 32 - 2 = - 36
